reasoning: The informal statement assumes G₁ and G₂ are cyclic groups "of orders m and n", which implicitly means they are finite with |G₁| = m and |G₂| = n. The Lean formalization makes this finiteness explicit via `[Fintype G] [Fintype H]`, and uses `IsCyclic G` / `IsCyclic H` hypotheses matching "G₁ and G₂ are cyclic groups". The conclusion `IsCyclic (G × H) ↔ (card G).Coprime (card H)` exactly captures "G₁ × G₂ is cyclic iff m and n are relatively prime" since `Fintype.card` yields the order of a finite group. The hypotheses and conclusion correspond one-to-one with the informal claim without strengthening or weakening.
